name: org.machanism.machai:project-layout
description: Project Layout is a Java library for detecting, describing, and processing conventional project directory layouts across multiple build and language ecosystems. The generated Javadoc describes it as a utility for build tooling, repository scanners, plugins, documentation generators, and automation workflows that need a uniform way to identify source directories, test directories, documentation inputs, module structures, project identifiers, and parent relationships. The core abstraction is org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout, an abstract model that returns root-relative paths for production sources, tests, documents, optional module directories, and project metadata such as project name, project id, layout type, and parent id. org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectLayoutManager is the main detection entry point and selects an appropriate implementation by inspecting a project directory, supporting Maven, Node.js-style package.json projects, Python projects, and a default fallback layout. org.machanism.machai.project.layout.MavenProjectLayout reads pom.xml metadata to resolve Maven modules, source directories, test directories, resources, documentation roots, project identifiers, and parent artifact information. org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor provides a reusable module-aware scanning workflow that detects layouts, recursively processes modules when present, and delegates custom folder handling to subclasses. The library also exposes filesystem-oriented helper methods such as org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout.findFiles(java.io.File), findDirectories(java.io.File), getRelativePath(java.io.File, java.io.File), and getExcludeDirs() to support repository crawling while excluding noisy build, cache, IDE, and dependency directories. Based on its Javadoc, this artifact is best classified as infrastructure and utility tooling for project-structure discovery, repository analysis, and module-aware automation in Java-based developer tools.

usageContext: Build plugins that need to locate source, test, and documentation roots
Repository scanners that must work across Maven, Node.js, Python, and generic project structures
Code indexing and embedding pipelines that require normalized project layout metadata
Module-aware automation for traversing multi-project repositories
Documentation generators and site processors that need root-relative content discovery
Developer tools that must ignore build, cache, IDE, and dependency directories during recursive scans

constructors.description: Primary creation entry point for obtaining a configured org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout. It inspects the provided directory and chooses the matching layout implementation for Maven, Node.js-style, Python, or generic projects so callers can immediately query sources, tests, documentation roots, modules, and metadata through a unified API.

examples.description: How to use it: create a java.io.File pointing to a repository root, call the detection method, and then query normalized source, test, and documentation directories without hardcoding ecosystem-specific conventions.

constructors.description: Creates the Maven-specific layout implementation. Use this when the caller already knows the project is Maven-based and wants direct access to Maven-aware source, test, documentation, module, project, and parent metadata derived from pom.xml.

examples.description: How to use it: instantiate the Maven layout directly, set the project directory, and then query Maven-derived directories and module definitions when auto-detection is unnecessary.

constructors.description: Creates the minimal fallback layout implementation. It is useful for generic repositories where language-specific build metadata is unavailable but callers still want lightweight module discovery based on subdirectories and a consistent layout abstraction.

examples.description: How to use it: construct the fallback layout for a repository with no supported build metadata and use it to inspect immediate subdirectory modules while receiving empty lists for non-inferred source and documentation roots.

features.name: detectProjectLayout
features.description: Detects the effective project layout implementation for a given directory. According to the Javadoc, it supports Maven, Node.js package.json projects, Python projects, and a generic default layout, making it the central feature for cross-ecosystem project structure recognition.

examples.description: How to use it: detect the layout for an arbitrary repository and then inspect its normalized metadata and conventional directories. This is the most practical starting point for indexing, reporting, and automation tools.

features.name: getSources
features.description: Returns root-relative production source directories for the active layout. This abstraction hides ecosystem-specific conventions and lets callers obtain implementation source roots from Maven or other supported project types through a single method.

examples.description: How to use it: resolve each returned root-relative entry against layout.getProjectDir() before scanning files. This pattern works well for static analysis, documentation generation, and repository indexing.

features.name: getTests
features.description: Returns root-relative test directories for the active layout. The method enables tools to distinguish test material from production sources without hardcoding build-system-specific directory names.

examples.description: How to use it: enumerate test roots and pass them into test-oriented workflows such as quality reports, coverage indexing, or exclusion logic for production-only scans.

features.name: getDocuments
features.description: Returns root-relative documentation directories for the active layout. In Maven projects this includes src/site, allowing documentation-oriented tooling to discover narrative content consistently.

examples.description: How to use it: collect documentation roots so your tool can process site content, project documentation, or auxiliary narrative files in a convention-aware way.

features.name: findFiles
features.description: Recursively lists files below a directory while excluding known build and tooling directories. This feature supports repository crawling with less noise from caches, generated outputs, IDE files, and dependency folders.

examples.description: How to use it: call the static helper on a repository root to get a filtered recursive listing that already omits noisy directories such as node_modules, .git, target, build, and similar tooling folders.

features.name: scanFolder
features.description: Provides a module-aware project scanning workflow. The method detects the effective layout, processes each module when modules are present, and otherwise processes the current folder, making it suitable for repository traversal utilities and build-time automation.

examples.description: How to use it: create a ProjectProcessor subclass, implement processFolder, and invoke scanFolder on a repository root. The library handles layout detection and module traversal before delegating your custom logic.

customizations.name: processFolder
customizations.description: Primary extension point for custom processing logic. Subclasses of org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor must implement processFolder(org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout) to define what should happen once a project or module layout has been detected.

examples.description: How to use it: override processFolder and implement your own indexing, validation, reporting, or transformation logic using the normalized ProjectLayout abstraction supplied by the library.

customizations.name: ProjectLayout
customizations.description: Abstract base type that can be extended to model unsupported or organization-specific repository conventions. Custom implementations can provide their own logic for modules, sources, tests, documents, project ids, names, and parent relationships while staying compatible with tooling built on the ProjectLayout API.

examples.description: How to use it: implement a custom layout when your repository structure does not match the built-in Maven, Node.js, Python, or default layout strategies.

examples.title: Use Project Layout in a Maven-based command line utility
examples.scenario: Install the library in a Maven project, configure a runnable application, and execute it against a repository to print layout type, source roots, test roots, and documentation roots.

steps.instruction: Install the library by adding the Maven dependency to your project.
Configure a runnable component. A simple approach is to use the Exec Maven Plugin so your Java main class behaves like a CLI utility.
Create a Java entry point that detects the project layout and prints the discovered directories.
Run the utility against a real repository directory.

examples.description: This practical how-to-use-it example covers installation, configuration, and execution of a ready-to-run Maven-based utility that consumes Project Layout as a library.

examples.title: Build a filtered repository scanner with the helper API
examples.scenario: Use the library's recursive file traversal helper to scan a repository while automatically excluding build, cache, IDE, and dependency directories.

steps.instruction: Install the library as a Maven dependency.
Configure a runnable Java application. The Exec Maven Plugin is a straightforward way to invoke a main class from Maven.
Run the scanner against a target repository.

examples.description: This how-to-use-it scenario demonstrates a practical developer utility that leverages the library's built-in directory exclusion behavior to keep recursive scans relevant and efficient.

examples.title: Create a custom module-aware processor
examples.scenario: Implement org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor to process each module or leaf project in a repository using the normalized ProjectLayout abstraction.

steps.instruction: Add the dependency to your Maven project.
Configure the project so you can run a Java main class from Maven.
Implement a ProjectProcessor that reports each processed layout.
Run the processor on a repository that may contain modules.

examples.description: This example shows how to install, configure, and run a reusable processor component that traverses a repository and handles each detected module or leaf project with a consistent API.
